meetings.title: Technical Troubleshooting — Acme Corp

moments.text: Priya asked if we support a Snowflake connector. Tom said no — but 'we promised it was on the roadmap 6 months ago.' No ETA exists.
Raj found a data integrity issue: duplicate records appearing after their CSV import. Affects ~2000 records. Manual cleanup required.
Raj reported the API is returning 504 timeouts on bulk exports. Affecting their nightly data sync — been happening for 2 weeks.
Priya escalated directly to Marcus about the API rate limits. They hit the cap during business hours — needs 10x increase or it's a blocker.
Tom mentioned they promised Acme a custom report builder that doesn't exist in our product. It was a pre-sales commitment from 8 months ago.
Onboarding call: walked through SSO setup. Acme uses Okta — our SAML integration had a redirect bug that Tom escalated to engineering.
Support ticket #4521: user permissions not syncing from Active Directory. Workaround in place but fragile. Raj wants a permanent fix.
Integration review: their Hubspot data flow is working but our webhook payloads are missing the custom field mappings they configured.
Tom committed to a dedicated support channel for Acme — Slack Connect. This was supposed to be set up 3 weeks ago but still pending.
Onboarding milestone: 80% of Acme users have logged in at least once. But only 35% are weekly active. Need engagement strategy.

meetings.title: Training Session — VertexAI

moments.text: Leo wants to roll out to their entire engineering org — 400 seats. Currently on 50. But he needs SSO and SCIM provisioning first.
Leo mentioned they're evaluating Notion and Confluence for their wiki — potential integration opportunity if we can embed our widgets.
Training session went well. 92% satisfaction score. But Leo flagged that the admin interface is 'confusing for non-technical team leads.'
Expansion pricing discussion: Leo wants a volume discount at 400 seats. Current per-seat price is $45/mo — he's asking for $30.
Dana proposed a customer advisory board seat for Leo. He'd get early access to features in exchange for regular feedback.
Maria reported that their data science team built custom dashboards on top of our API. They want official support for that use case.
Usage review: VertexAI's power users average 4.2 hours/day in the product. But 60% of users log in less than once a week.
Maria asked about our SOC 2 report — VertexAI's compliance team needs it for their vendor audit. We sent it over same day.

meetings.title: Security Review — Globex Industries

moments.text: Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
Victor mentioned they'd want a dedicated solutions engineer for the first 90 days post-launch. We don't currently offer that tier of support.
Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.
Discovery: Globex processes 2M transactions/day. Their current system (built in-house) can't scale past 3M. That's their pain.
Elena flagged data residency requirements — all data must stay in EU. We currently only have US regions. This could be a dealbreaker.
Elena requires SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance before any POC. We have SOC 2 but HIPAA is in progress — ETA unclear.
Nina discovered that Globex's legacy system uses a proprietary data format. Migration will need a custom ETL pipeline — 4-6 weeks of eng work.
Demo went well. Victor said 'this is the first product that actually handles our multi-tenant architecture requirements natively.'
Victor wants to see our system handle their peak load scenario: 50k concurrent users, 500 API calls/second. Nina is setting up a load test.
POC results: 99.7% accuracy on their test dataset. Victor is impressed but wants to test edge cases around Unicode handling.
Security deep dive complete. Elena signed off on our encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3) but wants us to add FIPS 140-2 certification.
Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
Victor mentioned they'd want a dedicated solutions engineer for the first 90 days post-launch. We don't currently offer that tier of support.
Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.

meetings.summary: Security Review covering demo, POC, pilot topics.

meetings.title: Design Review — Internal (Product Strategy)

moments.text: Marcus pushed for the Salesforce integration to be moved up in priority — it's blocking the Acme renewal. Lena pushed back on scope creep.
OKR review: Product hit 3 of 5 key results. Missed on 'reduce time-to-value for new customers' and 'launch mobile app beta.'
Chris flagged technical debt in the auth module. Refactor would take 2 sprints but would prevent recurring security patches.
Design review for the new dashboard. Yuki proposed a widget-based layout. Chris pushed back — too much complexity for v1.
Release 4.2 postmortem: the rollout caused a 45-minute outage due to a database migration script that wasn't tested on production-scale data.
Backlog grooming: 127 items. Lena is ruthlessly cutting — anything not tied to a revenue goal or a top-10 customer request gets archived.
Yuki demoed the new onboarding flow. Reduced steps from 12 to 5. A/B test shows 30% improvement in completion rate.
Feature request triage: 42 requests from customers this month. Top 3: webhook improvements, bulk export, and role-based permissions.
Sprint retro: velocity dropped 20% because of unplanned Acme support escalations pulling engineers off feature work.
Lena presented the Q3 roadmap: AI summarization, advanced analytics, and the Snowflake connector. Chris said engineering capacity only covers 2 of the 3.

meetings.title: Executive Save Meeting — Zenith Health

moments.text: Dana presented a save plan: dedicated support engineer, weekly check-ins, and a 15% discount on renewal. Marcus approved.
Escalation call: Karen's team found a data export that was missing 3 months of records. Potential compliance issue for a healthcare company.
Zenith's usage is actually increasing despite the complaints — their team is locked in. The frustration is real but switching cost is high.
Tom's team traced the bug to a race condition in the sync engine. Fix is in staging but needs 2 more weeks of testing before production.
Karen said their contract auto-renews in 60 days. If the issues aren't resolved by then, they'll invoke the termination clause.
Health check reveals Zenith's NPS dropped from 42 to -8 in the last quarter. Red alert.
Karen expressed frustration: 'We've reported the same bug 4 times and it's still not fixed. My team has lost confidence in the product.'
Marcus had a private call with Karen's CEO. The CEO is pragmatic — willing to stay if we commit to a formal SLA with penalties.

meetings.title: QBR — Acme Corp

moments.text: Dana reviewed usage metrics — Acme's adoption dropped 15% last quarter. Three teams stopped using the product entirely.
Sarah identified an expansion opportunity in Acme's APAC division — 200 potential new seats if we can support Japanese localization.
James mentioned they're evaluating two competitors for the renewal. Price is the main concern — he said our per-seat cost is 40% above Competitor X.
Sarah suggested a joint case study to strengthen the relationship. Acme's marketing team is interested but legal needs to approve.
Priya wants a native Salesforce integration before they'll commit to a 3-year deal. She said their team spends 2 hours daily on manual data entry.
Marcus offered to fly out for an in-person executive dinner. Trying to get face time with their CEO who hasn't been in any calls.
Pricing discussion got heated. James said 'at this price point, I can get 80% of the functionality from a competitor at half the cost.'
Priya asked about our AI roadmap. She's concerned we're falling behind on AI features compared to competitors.
Marcus committed to having the integration ready by February. Engineering hasn't been looped in on this timeline yet.
Dana presented a health score of 62/100 for Acme. Red flags: declining logins, support sentiment negative, champion (Priya) updating LinkedIn.
The CFO flagged that their fiscal year ends in March. Any renewal needs to close by end of Q1 or it gets pushed to next budget cycle.
End of call: James said 'I like your product, but I need ammunition to defend this spend to my board.' We need to build that ammo.
James revealed they're consolidating vendors — going from 8 tools to 4. We need to be in that final 4 or we're out.
Sarah proposed a volume discount: 20% off if they expand to 500 seats. James seemed interested but needs board approval.
Priya wants to pilot our new analytics module. If it works well, that could be the lever to justify the renewal price.
James wants to renegotiate the SLA terms. Current 99.9% uptime guarantee isn't being met — they had 3 outages last quarter.

meetings.title: Security Review — Globex Industries

moments.text: Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
Victor wants to see our system handle their peak load scenario: 50k concurrent users, 500 API calls/second. Nina is setting up a load test.
Demo went well. Victor said 'this is the first product that actually handles our multi-tenant architecture requirements natively.'
Victor mentioned they'd want a dedicated solutions engineer for the first 90 days post-launch. We don't currently offer that tier of support.
Elena flagged data residency requirements — all data must stay in EU. We currently only have US regions. This could be a dealbreaker.
POC results: 99.7% accuracy on their test dataset. Victor is impressed but wants to test edge cases around Unicode handling.
Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.
Nina discovered that Globex's legacy system uses a proprietary data format. Migration will need a custom ETL pipeline — 4-6 weeks of eng work.
Elena requires SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance before any POC. We have SOC 2 but HIPAA is in progress — ETA unclear.
Security deep dive complete. Elena signed off on our encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3) but wants us to add FIPS 140-2 certification.
Discovery: Globex processes 2M transactions/day. Their current system (built in-house) can't scale past 3M. That's their pain.

meetings.title: Pricing Review — Acme Corp

moments.text: Pricing discussion got heated. James said 'at this price point, I can get 80% of the functionality from a competitor at half the cost.'
The CFO flagged that their fiscal year ends in March. Any renewal needs to close by end of Q1 or it gets pushed to next budget cycle.
Priya asked about our AI roadmap. She's concerned we're falling behind on AI features compared to competitors.
Dana reviewed usage metrics — Acme's adoption dropped 15% last quarter. Three teams stopped using the product entirely.
Marcus offered to fly out for an in-person executive dinner. Trying to get face time with their CEO who hasn't been in any calls.
Dana presented a health score of 62/100 for Acme. Red flags: declining logins, support sentiment negative, champion (Priya) updating LinkedIn.
Sarah suggested a joint case study to strengthen the relationship. Acme's marketing team is interested but legal needs to approve.
James revealed they're consolidating vendors — going from 8 tools to 4. We need to be in that final 4 or we're out.
The renewal discussion stalled when James brought up the unresolved support tickets. 12 tickets open for more than 30 days.
James mentioned they're evaluating two competitors for the renewal. Price is the main concern — he said our per-seat cost is 40% above Competitor X.
Sarah identified an expansion opportunity in Acme's APAC division — 200 potential new seats if we can support Japanese localization.
Priya wants to pilot our new analytics module. If it works well, that could be the lever to justify the renewal price.
Priya's team built a workaround for the missing webhook feature. She said if we don't ship it by Q2, they'll build their own middleware.
Marcus committed to having the integration ready by February. Engineering hasn't been looped in on this timeline yet.
Sarah proposed a volume discount: 20% off if they expand to 500 seats. James seemed interested but needs board approval.
James wants to renegotiate the SLA terms. Current 99.9% uptime guarantee isn't being met — they had 3 outages last quarter.
End of call: James said 'I like your product, but I need ammunition to defend this spend to my board.' We need to build that ammo.

Backlog grooming: 127 items. Lena is ruthlessly cutting — anything not tied to a revenue goal or a top-10 customer request gets archived.

Marcus pushed for the Salesforce integration to be moved up in priority — it's blocking the Acme renewal. Lena pushed back on scope creep.
Sprint retro: velocity dropped 20% because of unplanned Acme support escalations pulling engineers off feature work.

moments.text: Marcus had a private call with Karen's CEO. The CEO is pragmatic — willing to stay if we commit to a formal SLA with penalties.

meetings.title: Executive Alignment — Acme Corp

moments.text: Priya wants a native Salesforce integration before they'll commit to a 3-year deal. She said their team spends 2 hours daily on manual data entry.
Dana reviewed usage metrics — Acme's adoption dropped 15% last quarter. Three teams stopped using the product entirely.
Pricing discussion got heated. James said 'at this price point, I can get 80% of the functionality from a competitor at half the cost.'
The CFO flagged that their fiscal year ends in March. Any renewal needs to close by end of Q1 or it gets pushed to next budget cycle.
James wants to renegotiate the SLA terms. Current 99.9% uptime guarantee isn't being met — they had 3 outages last quarter.
Sarah identified an expansion opportunity in Acme's APAC division — 200 potential new seats if we can support Japanese localization.
Marcus committed to having the integration ready by February. Engineering hasn't been looped in on this timeline yet.
End of call: James said 'I like your product, but I need ammunition to defend this spend to my board.' We need to build that ammo.
The renewal discussion stalled when James brought up the unresolved support tickets. 12 tickets open for more than 30 days.
Priya's team built a workaround for the missing webhook feature. She said if we don't ship it by Q2, they'll build their own middleware.
James mentioned they're evaluating two competitors for the renewal. Price is the main concern — he said our per-seat cost is 40% above Competitor X.

James revealed they're consolidating vendors — going from 8 tools to 4. We need to be in that final 4 or we're out.

meetings.title: Security Review — Globex Industries

moments.text: Nina discovered that Globex's legacy system uses a proprietary data format. Migration will need a custom ETL pipeline — 4-6 weeks of eng work.
Discovery: Globex processes 2M transactions/day. Their current system (built in-house) can't scale past 3M. That's their pain.
Elena requires SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance before any POC. We have SOC 2 but HIPAA is in progress — ETA unclear.
Victor wants to see our system handle their peak load scenario: 50k concurrent users, 500 API calls/second. Nina is setting up a load test.
Security deep dive complete. Elena signed off on our encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3) but wants us to add FIPS 140-2 certification.
POC results: 99.7% accuracy on their test dataset. Victor is impressed but wants to test edge cases around Unicode handling.
Elena flagged data residency requirements — all data must stay in EU. We currently only have US regions. This could be a dealbreaker.
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
Demo went well. Victor said 'this is the first product that actually handles our multi-tenant architecture requirements natively.'
Victor mentioned they'd want a dedicated solutions engineer for the first 90 days post-launch. We don't currently offer that tier of support.
Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.
Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.

meetings.title: QBR — Acme Corp

moments.text: Dana presented a health score of 62/100 for Acme. Red flags: declining logins, support sentiment negative, champion (Priya) updating LinkedIn.
Priya wants a native Salesforce integration before they'll commit to a 3-year deal. She said their team spends 2 hours daily on manual data entry.
Sarah identified an expansion opportunity in Acme's APAC division — 200 potential new seats if we can support Japanese localization.
End of call: James said 'I like your product, but I need ammunition to defend this spend to my board.' We need to build that ammo.
Sarah proposed a volume discount: 20% off if they expand to 500 seats. James seemed interested but needs board approval.
Pricing discussion got heated. James said 'at this price point, I can get 80% of the functionality from a competitor at half the cost.'
James revealed they're consolidating vendors — going from 8 tools to 4. We need to be in that final 4 or we're out.
James mentioned they're evaluating two competitors for the renewal. Price is the main concern — he said our per-seat cost is 40% above Competitor X.
The CFO flagged that their fiscal year ends in March. Any renewal needs to close by end of Q1 or it gets pushed to next budget cycle.
Marcus committed to having the integration ready by February. Engineering hasn't been looped in on this timeline yet.
Priya asked about our AI roadmap. She's concerned we're falling behind on AI features compared to competitors.
Priya wants to pilot our new analytics module. If it works well, that could be the lever to justify the renewal price.
Marcus offered to fly out for an in-person executive dinner. Trying to get face time with their CEO who hasn't been in any calls.
James wants to renegotiate the SLA terms. Current 99.9% uptime guarantee isn't being met — they had 3 outages last quarter.
The renewal discussion stalled when James brought up the unresolved support tickets. 12 tickets open for more than 30 days.
Dana reviewed usage metrics — Acme's adoption dropped 15% last quarter. Three teams stopped using the product entirely.
Sarah suggested a joint case study to strengthen the relationship. Acme's marketing team is interested but legal needs to approve.
Priya's team built a workaround for the missing webhook feature. She said if we don't ship it by Q2, they'll build their own middleware.

meetings.title: Technical Deep Dive — Globex Industries

moments.text: Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.
Nina discovered that Globex's legacy system uses a proprietary data format. Migration will need a custom ETL pipeline — 4-6 weeks of eng work.
Victor wants to see our system handle their peak load scenario: 50k concurrent users, 500 API calls/second. Nina is setting up a load test.
Elena requires SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance before any POC. We have SOC 2 but HIPAA is in progress — ETA unclear.
Demo went well. Victor said 'this is the first product that actually handles our multi-tenant architecture requirements natively.'
Victor mentioned they'd want a dedicated solutions engineer for the first 90 days post-launch. We don't currently offer that tier of support.
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
POC results: 99.7% accuracy on their test dataset. Victor is impressed but wants to test edge cases around Unicode handling.
Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.
Discovery: Globex processes 2M transactions/day. Their current system (built in-house) can't scale past 3M. That's their pain.
Elena flagged data residency requirements — all data must stay in EU. We currently only have US regions. This could be a dealbreaker.
Security deep dive complete. Elena signed off on our encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3) but wants us to add FIPS 140-2 certification.

meetings.title: Adoption Review — VertexAI

moments.text: Maria asked about our SOC 2 report — VertexAI's compliance team needs it for their vendor audit. We sent it over same day.
Leo wants to roll out to their entire engineering org — 400 seats. Currently on 50. But he needs SSO and SCIM provisioning first.
Expansion pricing discussion: Leo wants a volume discount at 400 seats. Current per-seat price is $45/mo — he's asking for $30.
Leo mentioned they're evaluating Notion and Confluence for their wiki — potential integration opportunity if we can embed our widgets.
Maria reported that their data science team built custom dashboards on top of our API. They want official support for that use case.
Dana proposed a customer advisory board seat for Leo. He'd get early access to features in exchange for regular feedback.
Training session went well. 92% satisfaction score. But Leo flagged that the admin interface is 'confusing for non-technical team leads.'
Usage review: VertexAI's power users average 4.2 hours/day in the product. But 60% of users log in less than once a week.

meetings.title: Executive Save Meeting — Zenith Health

moments.text: Marcus had a private call with Karen's CEO. The CEO is pragmatic — willing to stay if we commit to a formal SLA with penalties.
Karen expressed frustration: 'We've reported the same bug 4 times and it's still not fixed. My team has lost confidence in the product.'
Dana presented a save plan: dedicated support engineer, weekly check-ins, and a 15% discount on renewal. Marcus approved.
Health check reveals Zenith's NPS dropped from 42 to -8 in the last quarter. Red alert.
Karen said their contract auto-renews in 60 days. If the issues aren't resolved by then, they'll invoke the termination clause.
Tom's team traced the bug to a race condition in the sync engine. Fix is in staging but needs 2 more weeks of testing before production.
Escalation call: Karen's team found a data export that was missing 3 months of records. Potential compliance issue for a healthcare company.
Zenith's usage is actually increasing despite the complaints — their team is locked in. The frustration is real but switching cost is high.

meetings.title: Positioning Workshop — Internal (Competitive Intelligence)

moments.text: Sarah shared intel from a prospect: CompetitorX is offering 6-month free pilots to enterprise accounts. Predatory pricing.
CompetitorY just raised $50M Series C. They're hiring aggressively in our target market — poaching our customers' contacts.
Aisha reported that CompetitorZ is spreading FUD about our uptime. Shared a slide claiming we had '47 hours of downtime last year' — it's false.
Marcus wants a 'competitive SWAT team' — 2 SEs who only work on competitive deals. Chris says we can't spare the headcount.
Win-loss analysis: lost 3 deals to CompetitorX last quarter. All cited 'better AI features' and 'lower price point' as reasons.
Battlecard review: our positioning on security is strong but we're weak on 'time-to-value' messaging. Customers say onboarding takes too long.
Market analysis: the total addressable market grew 25% YoY. But 3 new entrants launched this quarter — the space is getting crowded.

meetings.title: Renewal Discussion — Acme Corp

moments.text: The renewal discussion stalled when James brought up the unresolved support tickets. 12 tickets open for more than 30 days.
Marcus offered to fly out for an in-person executive dinner. Trying to get face time with their CEO who hasn't been in any calls.
Dana reviewed usage metrics — Acme's adoption dropped 15% last quarter. Three teams stopped using the product entirely.
Priya wants a native Salesforce integration before they'll commit to a 3-year deal. She said their team spends 2 hours daily on manual data entry.
Priya asked about our AI roadmap. She's concerned we're falling behind on AI features compared to competitors.
Sarah suggested a joint case study to strengthen the relationship. Acme's marketing team is interested but legal needs to approve.
Priya's team built a workaround for the missing webhook feature. She said if we don't ship it by Q2, they'll build their own middleware.
James wants to renegotiate the SLA terms. Current 99.9% uptime guarantee isn't being met — they had 3 outages last quarter.
James mentioned they're evaluating two competitors for the renewal. Price is the main concern — he said our per-seat cost is 40% above Competitor X.
Sarah proposed a volume discount: 20% off if they expand to 500 seats. James seemed interested but needs board approval.
Sarah identified an expansion opportunity in Acme's APAC division — 200 potential new seats if we can support Japanese localization.
Marcus committed to having the integration ready by February. Engineering hasn't been looped in on this timeline yet.
End of call: James said 'I like your product, but I need ammunition to defend this spend to my board.' We need to build that ammo.
The CFO flagged that their fiscal year ends in March. Any renewal needs to close by end of Q1 or it gets pushed to next budget cycle.
Dana presented a health score of 62/100 for Acme. Red flags: declining logins, support sentiment negative, champion (Priya) updating LinkedIn.
Pricing discussion got heated. James said 'at this price point, I can get 80% of the functionality from a competitor at half the cost.'
Priya wants to pilot our new analytics module. If it works well, that could be the lever to justify the renewal price.
James revealed they're consolidating vendors — going from 8 tools to 4. We need to be in that final 4 or we're out.

moments.text: Tom mentioned they promised Acme a custom report builder that doesn't exist in our product. It was a pre-sales commitment from 8 months ago.
Raj found a data integrity issue: duplicate records appearing after their CSV import. Affects ~2000 records. Manual cleanup required.
Integration review: their Hubspot data flow is working but our webhook payloads are missing the custom field mappings they configured.
Priya asked if we support a Snowflake connector. Tom said no — but 'we promised it was on the roadmap 6 months ago.' No ETA exists.
Tom committed to a dedicated support channel for Acme — Slack Connect. This was supposed to be set up 3 weeks ago but still pending.

meetings.title: Discovery Call — Globex Industries

moments.text: Nina discovered that Globex's legacy system uses a proprietary data format. Migration will need a custom ETL pipeline — 4-6 weeks of eng work.
Security deep dive complete. Elena signed off on our encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3) but wants us to add FIPS 140-2 certification.
Victor wants to see our system handle their peak load scenario: 50k concurrent users, 500 API calls/second. Nina is setting up a load test.
Victor mentioned they'd want a dedicated solutions engineer for the first 90 days post-launch. We don't currently offer that tier of support.
POC results: 99.7% accuracy on their test dataset. Victor is impressed but wants to test edge cases around Unicode handling.
Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
Elena flagged data residency requirements — all data must stay in EU. We currently only have US regions. This could be a dealbreaker.
Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.
Demo went well. Victor said 'this is the first product that actually handles our multi-tenant architecture requirements natively.'
Elena requires SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance before any POC. We have SOC 2 but HIPAA is in progress — ETA unclear.
Discovery: Globex processes 2M transactions/day. Their current system (built in-house) can't scale past 3M. That's their pain.

meetings.title: Rollout Planning — VertexAI

moments.text: Training session went well. 92% satisfaction score. But Leo flagged that the admin interface is 'confusing for non-technical team leads.'
Dana proposed a customer advisory board seat for Leo. He'd get early access to features in exchange for regular feedback.
Maria reported that their data science team built custom dashboards on top of our API. They want official support for that use case.
Expansion pricing discussion: Leo wants a volume discount at 400 seats. Current per-seat price is $45/mo — he's asking for $30.
Maria asked about our SOC 2 report — VertexAI's compliance team needs it for their vendor audit. We sent it over same day.
Leo wants to roll out to their entire engineering org — 400 seats. Currently on 50. But he needs SSO and SCIM provisioning first.
Usage review: VertexAI's power users average 4.2 hours/day in the product. But 60% of users log in less than once a week.
Leo mentioned they're evaluating Notion and Confluence for their wiki — potential integration opportunity if we can embed our widgets.

Nina discovered that Globex's legacy system uses a proprietary data format. Migration will need a custom ETL pipeline — 4-6 weeks of eng work.
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
Elena flagged data residency requirements — all data must stay in EU. We currently only have US regions. This could be a dealbreaker.
Demo went well. Victor said 'this is the first product that actually handles our multi-tenant architecture requirements natively.'
Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.
Security deep dive complete. Elena signed off on our encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3) but wants us to add FIPS 140-2 certification.
Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.
Elena requires SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance before any POC. We have SOC 2 but HIPAA is in progress — ETA unclear.

meetings.title: QBR — Acme Corp

moments.text: Dana presented a health score of 62/100 for Acme. Red flags: declining logins, support sentiment negative, champion (Priya) updating LinkedIn.
Dana reviewed usage metrics — Acme's adoption dropped 15% last quarter. Three teams stopped using the product entirely.
Marcus offered to fly out for an in-person executive dinner. Trying to get face time with their CEO who hasn't been in any calls.
End of call: James said 'I like your product, but I need ammunition to defend this spend to my board.' We need to build that ammo.
The CFO flagged that their fiscal year ends in March. Any renewal needs to close by end of Q1 or it gets pushed to next budget cycle.
James mentioned they're evaluating two competitors for the renewal. Price is the main concern — he said our per-seat cost is 40% above Competitor X.
Priya wants a native Salesforce integration before they'll commit to a 3-year deal. She said their team spends 2 hours daily on manual data entry.
James revealed they're consolidating vendors — going from 8 tools to 4. We need to be in that final 4 or we're out.
James wants to renegotiate the SLA terms. Current 99.9% uptime guarantee isn't being met — they had 3 outages last quarter.
Priya wants to pilot our new analytics module. If it works well, that could be the lever to justify the renewal price.
Sarah suggested a joint case study to strengthen the relationship. Acme's marketing team is interested but legal needs to approve.
Priya's team built a workaround for the missing webhook feature. She said if we don't ship it by Q2, they'll build their own middleware.
The renewal discussion stalled when James brought up the unresolved support tickets. 12 tickets open for more than 30 days.
Sarah identified an expansion opportunity in Acme's APAC division — 200 potential new seats if we can support Japanese localization.
Pricing discussion got heated. James said 'at this price point, I can get 80% of the functionality from a competitor at half the cost.'
Priya asked about our AI roadmap. She's concerned we're falling behind on AI features compared to competitors.
Sarah proposed a volume discount: 20% off if they expand to 500 seats. James seemed interested but needs board approval.

meetings.summary: QBR covering renewal, contract, pricing topics.

meetings.title: Rollout Planning — VertexAI

moments.text: Usage review: VertexAI's power users average 4.2 hours/day in the product. But 60% of users log in less than once a week.
Maria asked about our SOC 2 report — VertexAI's compliance team needs it for their vendor audit. We sent it over same day.
Expansion pricing discussion: Leo wants a volume discount at 400 seats. Current per-seat price is $45/mo — he's asking for $30.
Leo wants to roll out to their entire engineering org — 400 seats. Currently on 50. But he needs SSO and SCIM provisioning first.
Training session went well. 92% satisfaction score. But Leo flagged that the admin interface is 'confusing for non-technical team leads.'
Leo mentioned they're evaluating Notion and Confluence for their wiki — potential integration opportunity if we can embed our widgets.

meetings.title: Technical Troubleshooting — Acme Corp

moments.text: Support ticket #4521: user permissions not syncing from Active Directory. Workaround in place but fragile. Raj wants a permanent fix.
Priya asked if we support a Snowflake connector. Tom said no — but 'we promised it was on the roadmap 6 months ago.' No ETA exists.
Tom mentioned they promised Acme a custom report builder that doesn't exist in our product. It was a pre-sales commitment from 8 months ago.
Priya escalated directly to Marcus about the API rate limits. They hit the cap during business hours — needs 10x increase or it's a blocker.
Onboarding milestone: 80% of Acme users have logged in at least once. But only 35% are weekly active. Need engagement strategy.

meetings.title: Risk Review — Zenith Health

moments.text: Dana presented a save plan: dedicated support engineer, weekly check-ins, and a 15% discount on renewal. Marcus approved.
Escalation call: Karen's team found a data export that was missing 3 months of records. Potential compliance issue for a healthcare company.
Health check reveals Zenith's NPS dropped from 42 to -8 in the last quarter. Red alert.
Marcus had a private call with Karen's CEO. The CEO is pragmatic — willing to stay if we commit to a formal SLA with penalties.
Karen said their contract auto-renews in 60 days. If the issues aren't resolved by then, they'll invoke the termination clause.
Karen expressed frustration: 'We've reported the same bug 4 times and it's still not fixed. My team has lost confidence in the product.'
Zenith's usage is actually increasing despite the complaints — their team is locked in. The frustration is real but switching cost is high.
Tom's team traced the bug to a race condition in the sync engine. Fix is in staging but needs 2 more weeks of testing before production.

meetings.title: Executive Alignment — Acme Corp

moments.text: End of call: James said 'I like your product, but I need ammunition to defend this spend to my board.' We need to build that ammo.
Dana reviewed usage metrics — Acme's adoption dropped 15% last quarter. Three teams stopped using the product entirely.
Marcus committed to having the integration ready by February. Engineering hasn't been looped in on this timeline yet.
Dana presented a health score of 62/100 for Acme. Red flags: declining logins, support sentiment negative, champion (Priya) updating LinkedIn.
James revealed they're consolidating vendors — going from 8 tools to 4. We need to be in that final 4 or we're out.
Sarah proposed a volume discount: 20% off if they expand to 500 seats. James seemed interested but needs board approval.
Priya wants a native Salesforce integration before they'll commit to a 3-year deal. She said their team spends 2 hours daily on manual data entry.
Pricing discussion got heated. James said 'at this price point, I can get 80% of the functionality from a competitor at half the cost.'
The CFO flagged that their fiscal year ends in March. Any renewal needs to close by end of Q1 or it gets pushed to next budget cycle.
Priya asked about our AI roadmap. She's concerned we're falling behind on AI features compared to competitors.
James wants to renegotiate the SLA terms. Current 99.9% uptime guarantee isn't being met — they had 3 outages last quarter.
Priya's team built a workaround for the missing webhook feature. She said if we don't ship it by Q2, they'll build their own middleware.
James mentioned they're evaluating two competitors for the renewal. Price is the main concern — he said our per-seat cost is 40% above Competitor X.
Sarah suggested a joint case study to strengthen the relationship. Acme's marketing team is interested but legal needs to approve.
Sarah identified an expansion opportunity in Acme's APAC division — 200 potential new seats if we can support Japanese localization.
Priya wants to pilot our new analytics module. If it works well, that could be the lever to justify the renewal price.

meetings.summary: Executive Alignment covering renewal, contract, pricing topics.

meetings.title: POC Kickoff — Globex Industries

moments.text: Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.
Victor mentioned they'd want a dedicated solutions engineer for the first 90 days post-launch. We don't currently offer that tier of support.
Discovery: Globex processes 2M transactions/day. Their current system (built in-house) can't scale past 3M. That's their pain.
Demo went well. Victor said 'this is the first product that actually handles our multi-tenant architecture requirements natively.'
Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.
POC results: 99.7% accuracy on their test dataset. Victor is impressed but wants to test edge cases around Unicode handling.
Victor wants to see our system handle their peak load scenario: 50k concurrent users, 500 API calls/second. Nina is setting up a load test.
Elena flagged data residency requirements — all data must stay in EU. We currently only have US regions. This could be a dealbreaker.
Security deep dive complete. Elena signed off on our encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3) but wants us to add FIPS 140-2 certification.
Nina discovered that Globex's legacy system uses a proprietary data format. Migration will need a custom ETL pipeline — 4-6 weeks of eng work.
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
Elena requires SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance before any POC. We have SOC 2 but HIPAA is in progress — ETA unclear.

meetings.title: Technical Review — Acme Corp

moments.text: James mentioned they're evaluating two competitors for the renewal. Price is the main concern — he said our per-seat cost is 40% above Competitor X.
Marcus offered to fly out for an in-person executive dinner. Trying to get face time with their CEO who hasn't been in any calls.
Dana reviewed usage metrics — Acme's adoption dropped 15% last quarter. Three teams stopped using the product entirely.
Priya wants to pilot our new analytics module. If it works well, that could be the lever to justify the renewal price.
Priya asked about our AI roadmap. She's concerned we're falling behind on AI features compared to competitors.
Sarah suggested a joint case study to strengthen the relationship. Acme's marketing team is interested but legal needs to approve.
Priya wants a native Salesforce integration before they'll commit to a 3-year deal. She said their team spends 2 hours daily on manual data entry.
James revealed they're consolidating vendors — going from 8 tools to 4. We need to be in that final 4 or we're out.
Dana presented a health score of 62/100 for Acme. Red flags: declining logins, support sentiment negative, champion (Priya) updating LinkedIn.
Sarah proposed a volume discount: 20% off if they expand to 500 seats. James seemed interested but needs board approval.
The CFO flagged that their fiscal year ends in March. Any renewal needs to close by end of Q1 or it gets pushed to next budget cycle.
Priya's team built a workaround for the missing webhook feature. She said if we don't ship it by Q2, they'll build their own middleware.
Pricing discussion got heated. James said 'at this price point, I can get 80% of the functionality from a competitor at half the cost.'
Sarah identified an expansion opportunity in Acme's APAC division — 200 potential new seats if we can support Japanese localization.
Marcus committed to having the integration ready by February. Engineering hasn't been looped in on this timeline yet.
End of call: James said 'I like your product, but I need ammunition to defend this spend to my board.' We need to build that ammo.
The renewal discussion stalled when James brought up the unresolved support tickets. 12 tickets open for more than 30 days.

meetings.title: Risk Review — Zenith Health

moments.text: Health check reveals Zenith's NPS dropped from 42 to -8 in the last quarter. Red alert.
Tom's team traced the bug to a race condition in the sync engine. Fix is in staging but needs 2 more weeks of testing before production.
Zenith's usage is actually increasing despite the complaints — their team is locked in. The frustration is real but switching cost is high.
Marcus had a private call with Karen's CEO. The CEO is pragmatic — willing to stay if we commit to a formal SLA with penalties.
Dana presented a save plan: dedicated support engineer, weekly check-ins, and a 15% discount on renewal. Marcus approved.
Karen said their contract auto-renews in 60 days. If the issues aren't resolved by then, they'll invoke the termination clause.
Karen expressed frustration: 'We've reported the same bug 4 times and it's still not fixed. My team has lost confidence in the product.'
Escalation call: Karen's team found a data export that was missing 3 months of records. Potential compliance issue for a healthcare company.

meetings.title: Security Review — Globex Industries

moments.text: Demo went well. Victor said 'this is the first product that actually handles our multi-tenant architecture requirements natively.'
Procurement sent over a 47-page security questionnaire. Nina and Tom are splitting it — due back in 2 weeks.
POC results: 99.7% accuracy on their test dataset. Victor is impressed but wants to test edge cases around Unicode handling.
Discovery: Globex processes 2M transactions/day. Their current system (built in-house) can't scale past 3M. That's their pain.
Globex's legal team wants a custom DPA. Our standard one doesn't cover their specific sub-processor requirements.
Security deep dive complete. Elena signed off on our encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3) but wants us to add FIPS 140-2 certification.
Elena requires SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance before any POC. We have SOC 2 but HIPAA is in progress — ETA unclear.
Victor mentioned they'd want a dedicated solutions engineer for the first 90 days post-launch. We don't currently offer that tier of support.
Aisha learned Globex is also evaluating DataWeave and Segment. Timeline: decision by end of Q2. Budget: $800K ARR.
Nina discovered that Globex's legacy system uses a proprietary data format. Migration will need a custom ETL pipeline — 4-6 weeks of eng work.
Victor wants to see our system handle their peak load scenario: 50k concurrent users, 500 API calls/second. Nina is setting up a load test.
Elena flagged data residency requirements — all data must stay in EU. We currently only have US regions. This could be a dealbreaker.

CompetitorY just raised $50M Series C. They're hiring aggressively in our target market — poaching our customers' contacts.

Diversity hiring update: 40% of final-round candidates are from underrepresented groups, up from 25% last quarter.
Headcount planning: Marcus wants 3 more AEs and 1 SE for Q3. Angela said the budget only supports 2 hires total.
Angela raised concern about offer competitiveness. We lost 2 candidates to higher offers last month. Need to revisit comp bands.
Interview debrief for senior backend role. Candidate was strong technically but had concerns about our scale — compared us unfavorably to their current company.
Chris wants to hire a DevOps engineer to reduce deployment friction. Currently engineers spend 15% of time on infra issues.
Lena proposed creating a new 'Growth PM' role focused entirely on activation and retention metrics. Marcus supports it.

meetings.summary: Interview Debrief covering hiring, candidate, interview topics.

moments.text: Lena proposed an API-first partner program: give partners early access to new APIs and a rev-share on integrations they build.
Co-sell opportunity with Salesforce. Their AE has a prospect (NovaTech) who needs our capabilities. Could be $200K deal.
Intro call with Zapier: they want us in their top-tier integration directory. Requirements: OAuth 2.0 support and a published API schema.
Marketplace listing review: our Atlassian marketplace app has 340 installs but only 12 reviews. Need to drive more engagement.
Tom mentioned we promised CloudBridge a custom SSO adapter 5 months ago. It was a partnership commitment. Nobody built it.

Integration planning with Slack: they want us to build a Slack bot for in-channel notifications. Would differentiate us from competitors.
